metaData.Ingredients: Chicken, Wheat Flour, Cabbage, Water, Pork Fat, Modified Starch, Spring Onion, Soy Sauce [Soybean, Sugar, Water, Salt Flavour Enhancer (635)], Soybean Paste (Soybean, Salt, Water, Wheat Flour, Rice Flour, Sugar, Soybean Oil, Garlic, Shallot), Carrot, Sugar, Acidity Regulator (E262(i), E330, 500(i)), Sesame Oil, Salt, Black Pepper, Flavour Enhancer (640), Dextrin, Kombu Seaweed
metaData.Key Information:
- Quality Chicken & Vegetables Wrapped in a Thin Skin
- Quick & Convenient Reheathing via Multiple Methods (Microwave, Boil, Steam, Air Fry, Pan Fry)
- Ready in Under 3 Minutes
metaData.Preparation:
• Microwave:
1. Place Gyoza on a microwave dish
2. Add water to reach 1/4 of the gyoza
3. Cover and microwave for 2 to 2.30 minutes at 800 watts
4. Discard water after cooking
• Pan fry:
1. Add 1 teaspoon of vegetable oil into a frying pan and arrange Gyoza into pan
2. Cook over medium heat until bottoms are golden brown
3. Add 1/3 cup of water into frying pan and cover for about 5 minutes
4. Remove cover once water has evaporated and cook until the bottoms are brown and crispy
• Deep fry:
Deep fry Gyoza at 170°C to 180°C for 2.30 to 3 minutes or until golden brown
• Steam:
Steam Gyoza in tray at 90°C to 95°C for 7 to 8 minutes
• Boil:
Boil Gyoza in hot water or soup for 2.30 to 3 minutes
metaData.Storage Information:
• Keep frozen at -18°C or below
• Do not refreeze after defrosting

metaData.Ingredients: Wheat Flour, Pork, Water, Chinese Cabbage, Fully hydrogenated Palm Oil, Egg, Leek, Soy Sauce (Sugar, Water, Soybean, Salt), Modified Starch (E1412), Seasoning Powder {Salt, Maltodextrin (Cornstarch), Flavour Enhancers (E621, E627, E631), Cornstarch, Sugar, Chicken Meat and Fat, Flavoring, Soy Sauce [Soybean, Wheat, Salt, Maltodextrin, Modified Starch (E1440), E621, E917], Fully Hydrogenerated Palm Fat and Spice}, Ginger, Salt, Sesame Oil, Sugar, Yeast Extract, E339(iii) and Spices
metaData.Key Information:
• No Artificial Preservatives
• No Artificial Colouring
metaData.Nutritional Data:
Attributes Per Serving
Energy 200kcal
Protein 8.6g
Total Fat 6.9g
Saturated Fat 2.7g
Trans Fat 0g
Cholesterol 11mg
Carbohydrate 26.1mg
Total Sugar 3g
Dietary Fibre 2g
Sodium 348mg
metaData.Preparation:
• Cooking Instruction:
1. Boil - Bring the water to a boil in a pot and add the frozen dumplings. Stir gently to prevent the dumplings from shrinking together. Boil over medium heat for 5-7 minutes or until the dumplings float on top of the water.
2. Steam - Place the frozen dumplings in a steamer and steam for 12 minutes
3. Pan Fry - Heat up some oil in a frying pan and lay in the frozen dumpling. Add some water and cover the pan with lid. Simmer over low heat for 7 minutes or until the base of the dumplings turn golden brown
4. Deep Fry - Heat up some oil in a frying pan and lay in the frozen dumplings. Slowly fry over low heat until the dumplings turn golden brown. Coat the dumpling with batter for a crispier taste
metaData.Storage Information: Keep frozen (-18°C & below). Once thawed, do not refreeze.

product.description:
Manufactured by: 
Nan Xiang Food Industries
8A Admiralty Street, #05-30, Foodxchange@Admiralty, Singapore 757437

metaData.Ingredients:
Wheat Flour, Pork, Cabbage, Salt, Sugar, Ginger, Vegetable Oil, Sesame Oil, Soy Sauce(Soya, Wheat, Salt, Water), Flavour Enhancer (621)

Allergen: Contains Wheat, Sesame, Soya
metaData.Preparation:
DO NOT DEFROST
Steam
Steam for 10-12 minutes in steamer till skin in soft.
Boil
Bring pot of water to boil & place dumplings inside, add half cup of water & wait for water to boil, stir to prevent dumplings from sticking. After water has boil, simmer at low fire for 4-6 minutes till dumplings floats to surface.
Pan-fried
Follow "Boil" instructions, then place dumplings into heated non-stick pan with oil. Pour small amount of water & cover pan, fry with low fire till bottom is golden-brown.

metaData.Storage Information: Store below -18°c, once thawed do not refreeze

product.description: Ajinomoto Frozen Food's Gyoza is the number 1 gyoza brand in Japan. Our frozen food selection is a harmonious marriage of Japanese tradition and modern convenience. We bring with us artistry of Japanese culinary heritage, crafting each dish with precision and authencity.

metaData.Ingredients:
Vegetables (Cabbage, Onion, Garlic Chives),Whear Flour, Pork,Granular Soy Protein(Defatted Soybean,Edible Vegetable Oil), Shortening (Edible Vegetable Oil,Edible Refined Processed Oil & Fat E322), Corn Starch, Rapeseed Oil, Sugar, Soy Sauce (Defatted Soybean, Salt,Wheat, Soy, Ehanol),Protein Hydrolysate, Salt, Sesame Oil, E621, Spices (Ginger, Salt, White Pepper), Flavoured Oil (Garlic Flavour Extract), Egg White, Powdered Wheat Protein,E 322,Powdered Soy Protein (Defatted Soybean, Vegetable Oil,E322), Soy Flour, E415,E412, Skimmed Milk.

Contains Pork
metaData.Key Information:
-No.1 selling Hane Gyoza Pork in Japan.
-Made in Japan, using Hokkaido Pork
- First Authentic Japanese Pork HaneGyoza in Singapore
- Easy to prepare and kitchen friendly: No "oil and water"
- Crispy Layer around Gyoza (Hane): Enjoy JPN authentic restaurant quality gyoza at the comfort of your own home.
- Big size satisfies consumer to enjoy meaty gyoza.
metaData.Preparation:
1)Take Frozen Gyoza from tray and place on pan. Then heat pan with medium heat.
2) Close the lid and cook around 6.00-6.30 minutes with medium heat.( Cooking times can be adjusted based on gas range.)
3)Take off the lid, continue heating until water is evaporated and batter has golden brown.
metaData.SAP Product Name: AJINOMOTO JAPANESE STYLE HANE GYOZA PORK 276G
metaData.Storage Information: Keep frozen, store at or below -18 degrees celsius

metaData.Ingredients:
• Filling: Cabbage, Pork, Bean Thread (Water, Sweet Potato Starch, Carrageean, Salt) Textured Soy Flour (Water, Soy Flour), Onion, Green Onion, Sugar, Monosodium Glutamate, Soy Sauce (Water, Wheat, Soybean, Salt), Garlic, Salt, Ginger. Autolyzed Yeast, Black Pepper

metaData.Key Information: Enjoy Korean authentic taste at home with CJ Bibigo Dumplings. It is easy to cook and prepare, you can either steam them up or boil them. It is suitable for every one of all ages, children, adults or elderly. You can eat it by on its own or it can be an accompaniment to your meals as a side dish.
metaData.Nutritional Data:
Attributes Per Serving (140g)
Calories 240kcal
Trans Fat 0g
Protein 11g
Total Fat 6g
Saturated Fat 2g
Cholesterol 20mg
Sodium 550mg
Carbohydrate 36g
Dietary Fibre 2g
Sugars 3g
metaData.Preparation:
• Steamed Gyoza:
1. Pour enough water into a steamer and boil
2. When the water boils, add the frozen gyoza and steam about 5 minutes
• Gyoza Soup:
1. Make beef broth using real beef or using any soup base
2. Put some vegetables as you like into the broth and let it cook
3. When water boils add frozen gyoza and cook about 4 more minutes
• Fried Gyoza:
1. Heat the pan for about 30 seconds on medium-high heat
2. Add about 2 - 3 tablespoons of cooking oil into the pan
3. Add 8 - 10 pieces of gyoza on the heated pan and cook to 4.5 minutes or let the wrapper turn golden brown color

offers.description: Buy 1 CJ Bibigo Dumplings - Pork & Vegetables @ $6.95

metaData.Ingredients: Vegetables 35% (Cabbage, Onion, Green onion, Leek, Garlic, Cilantro), Wheat flour 16%, Water 15%, Fish cake 11%, Shortening, Textured soy protein, Modified tapioca starch, Tofu, Egg white, Wheat starch, Glass noodles, etc.
metaData.Key Information:
• Korean vegetables and seafood dumpling from Korea's No.1 dumpling brand, Bibigo
• Fish cake and 6 kinds of fresh vegetables, thin and wavy wrapper which holds extra flavor will provide you the best dumpling experience
metaData.Preparation:
• Pan-Fry
1. Pour 3 Tbsp of vegetable oil into a nonstick pan
2. Place 4 frozen Mandu pieces onto the pan, with spacing between them
3. Heat on medium-high heat for 8 minutes, turning each side every 2-3 minutes or until all sides are golden brown
• Steam
1. Pour enough water into a steamer and boil
2. Once the water is boiling, place 4 frozen Mandu pieces inside and steam for 8 minutes.
metaData.Storage Information: Keep Frozen (Quality may change if product is thawed then refrozen)
